author: kaka-srp
title: feat(agent-studio): add pack test publish flow (#177)
message:
feat(agent-studio): add pack test publish flow (#177)

* feat(agent-studio): add pack test publish flow

* fix(agent-studio): clarify pack test publish prompt
pr_body:
## Summary
- add Agent Studio `publish.py` flow for Pack Test start/status/submit
- upload candidate archives directly to the R2 access worker before creating Pack Test runs
- update Agent Studio prompts/docs for Accept-gated Pack Store submission and repeated test uploads
- add publish workflow unit tests

## Tests
- `python -m unittest discover -s .agents/skills/agent-studio/scripts/tests`
- `python -m py_compile .agents/skills/agent-studio/scripts/publish.py .agents/skills/agent-studio/scripts/tests/test_publish.py`

commit.message:
feat(agent-studio): add pack test publish flow (#177)

* feat(agent-studio): add pack test publish flow

* fix(agent-studio): clarify pack test publish prompt

@@ -15,7 +15,7 @@ Root .md files = Agent Studio identity. `agent/` = the built pack's .md files. S
 ## Truth sources (don't conflate)
 
 - `data/.scaffold-state.json` `agent_dir_filled` — "Stage 3 done?". Written by `scaffold.py` (false), `validate.py` (true when `check_agent_completeness` passes), and `import_archive.py` (true/false per the imported `agent/*.md`). Read by AGENTS.md's `/test` gate.
-- `data/.delivery-state.json` — "Stage 6 done?". Written by `install.py` / `run_share.py` on success, and by `import_archive.py` for `--share` imports (the artifacts URL proves prior delivery). Read by "Current stage" below.
+- `data/.delivery-state.json` — "Stage 6 done?". Written by `publish.py submit`, `install.py`, or `run_share.py` on success, and by `import_archive.py` for `--share` imports (the artifacts URL proves prior delivery). Read by "Current stage" below.
 - `validate.py` output — Stage 4 correctness check. Re-run as needed.
 - `data/config.json` — RUNTIME (deployed pack's end-user onboarding). **Not relevant to Studio's build progress.**
 - `data/spec-extract.json` — canonical machine source of truth for spec coverage (Studio writes; tooling reads). Present only when the creator provided a spec doc.

 - **Stage 2: Skill Design** — `references/skill-design.md`. **One skill at a time**, plan-before-code, update manifest after each.
 - **Stage 3: Agent Setup** — `references/automation.md`. Fill `agent/*.md` (SOUL/IDENTITY/AGENTS), design `quick_commands` per `references/quick-commands.md`, heartbeat/cron/onboarding, generate `pack-onboarding` skill.

+- **Stage 6: Delivery** — `references/delivery.md`. 6c Pack Store submit after Accept; 6a install and 6b share remain explicit legacy/side-channel choices.
 
 **Every stage ends with a GATE: present summary → ASK before the next.** Never auto-advance.
 
@@ -55,12 +55,12 @@ External data → `references/data-sources.md`'s 4-layer search. Never say "not
 
 > Bare script names below resolve to `uv run --python 3.12 <skill_root>/scripts/<name>.py`.
 
-- **`/dev`** / **`/test`** — switch mode (see AGENTS.md).
+- **`/dev`** / **`/test`** — switch mode (see AGENTS.md). `/test` is only local in-workspace functional simulation against `agent/`; never describe it as Pack Test, upload, staging install, or publish.
 - **`/studio new`** — reset workspace. (1) **Dirty check:** if any of `agent/agent-pack.yaml`, `skills/*/SKILL.md`, `scripts/*`, or `data/*` exists, the workspace is dirty. Read `name`+`version` from `agent/agent-pack.yaml` — if either is missing, ASK the creator for both (don't proceed to wipe without them). ASK to draft-snapshot (default Yes) → `snapshot.py capture --version <v> --draft --name <n>`. If creator declines, ASK to confirm "wipe in-progress work? This can't be undone." — only continue on explicit Yes. (2) `cron` `{"action":"list"}` → remove each test job. (3) `clean.py --confirm`. (4) Follow `BOOTSTRAP.md`.
 - **`/studio list`** — `list_packs.py`; render as a table (name · version · stage · last-touched · bio). Empty → offer `/dev` or `/studio new`.
 - **`/studio open [<target>]`** — resume a built pack. Dispatch on `<target>`: https URL or its base64 → `import_archive.py --share <target>`; local `.tar.gz`/`.tgz` path → `import_archive.py --archive <target>`; anything else → snapshot name (`snapshot.py restore --name <target>`). No `<target>` → `list_packs.py` + ASK which snapshot. Archive forms handle packs with no snapshot; import auto-captures one for next time. Flow: (1) resolve target; a snapshot name not in `list_packs.py` → list available and stop, don't fall back to archive. (2) refuse if `.mode == test` (`/dev` first); no-op if the snapshot is the current pack. (3) **dirty check** (as `/studio new`): if dirty, ASK to draft-snapshot, **refuse if declined**. (4) `clean.py --confirm`. (5) snapshot → `snapshot.py restore` (handles BOOTSTRAP→.done), archive → `import_archive.py`; surface any `warning` it returns. (6) read `agent/agent-pack.yaml` + `snapshots/<name>/context.md` if present. (7) report name + version + stage; ASK keep-iterating or bump. (8) review `quick_commands` before Testing/Packaging.
 - **`/studio validate`** — `validate.py --skip-online-validation` via subagent, plain-language report. (Creator-side check; CI release validates omit the flag.)
-- **`/studio publish`** — Stage 5 → §5f gate. Run `package.py --output zip/`, then `snapshot.py capture --version <v>` (archives editable state). Then ASK install / share / stop. See `references/packaging.md` §5e.
+- **`/studio publish`** — Stage 5 + Pack Test. Requires `CLAW_INTERFACE_URL`, `R2_AGENT_PACKS_UPLOAD_URL`, and `USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN`; never ask the creator for `org_id` because `publish.py` resolves it from `/account/me`. Run `publish.py start --output zip/`; it packages, snapshots, uploads a fresh candidate to the R2 access worker, creates a new Pack Test run, and shows `preview_url` + `test_run_id`. Do not auto-submit. After the creator tests the preview and clicks Accept, run `publish.py submit` to promote the accepted asset and create the Pack Store submission. If the creator is not satisfied, iterate and run `/studio publish` again; do not mention a creator Reject action. When offering this command to creators, describe it as "upload a candidate package and start a temporary Pack Test bot"; never say it "generates a share link", "installs to your account", or "publishes/lists to the store" before Accept. See `references/packaging.md` §5e-§5f and `references/delivery.md` §6c.
 - **`/studio share`** — route to `.agents/skills/agent-studio-share/SKILL.md`.
 - **`/studio install`** — route to `.agents/skills/agent-studio-install/SKILL.md`. Never invoke `openclaw ...` directly.

+- `/studio publish` — full Pack Test. It packages the archive, uploads it to the R2 access worker, creates a Pack Test run, and opens a temporary staging bot preview. It does **not** generate a share link or submit/list the pack until the creator accepts the preview.

@@ -0,0 +1,668 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python3
+"""Create, preview, and submit Agent Studio pack test runs.
+
+Flow:
+1. start  -> package current pack, upload archive to R2 worker, create backend pack test run, wait for preview URL
+2. status -> read the latest backend status for the saved or provided test run
+3. submit -> after creator accepts the preview, promote the asset and submit to Pack Store
+"""
+
+import argparse
+import hashlib
+import json
+import os
+import re
+import subprocess
+import sys
+import time
+import uuid
+
+from pathlib import Path
+from urllib import error, request
+
+from _common import (
+    DELIVERY_METHOD_PACK_STORE,
+    extract_field,
+    find_pack_manifest,
+    read_description_json,
+    write_delivery_state,
+)
+
+SCRIPT_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
+PACKAGE_SCRIPT = SCRIPT_DIR / "package.py"
+SNAPSHOT_SCRIPT = SCRIPT_DIR / "snapshot.py"
+STATE_REL = Path("data") / ".pack-publish-state.json"
+
+DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL = 5.0
+DEFAULT_POLL_TIMEOUT = 600.0
+TERMINAL_PREVIEW_STATUSES = {
+    "ready_for_preview",
+    "previewing",
+    "accepted",
+    "promoted",
+    "failed",
+    "timed_out",
+    "cleaned",
+}
+SUBMIT_READY_STATUSES = {"accepted", "promoted"}
+
+
+class PublishError(RuntimeError):
+    """Raised for expected publish workflow failures."""
+
+
+def main() -> None:
+    parser = build_parser()
+    args = parser.parse_args()
+    pack_dir = Path.cwd()
+
+    try:
+        client = BackendClient.from_env()
+        if args.command == "start":
+            upload_client = R2UploadClient.from_env(client.token)
+            payload = start_publish(pack_dir, client, upload_client, args)
+        elif args.command == "status":
+            payload = status_publish(pack_dir, client, args)
+        elif args.command == "submit":
+            payload = submit_publish(pack_dir, client, args)
+        else:
+            raise PublishError(f"Unsupported command: {args.command}")
+    except (PublishError, FileNotFoundError, ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:
+        json.dump({"status": "error", "message": str(exc)}, sys.stdout, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
+        sys.exit(1)
+
+    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
+
+
+def build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Publish current Agent Studio pack through Pack Test")
+    subparsers = parser.add_subparsers(dest="command", required=True)
+
+    start_parser = subparsers.add_parser("start", help="Package and create a Pack Test run")
+    start_parser.add_argument("--output", default="zip/", help="Archive directory")
+    start_parser.add_argument("--poll-interval", type=float, default=DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL)
+    start_parser.add_argument("--poll-timeout", type=float, default=DEFAULT_POLL_TIMEOUT)
+    start_parser.add_argument("--skip-snapshot", action="store_true", help="Do not capture an editable snapshot")
+    start_parser.add_argument(
+        "--skip-connector-validation",
+        action="store_true",
+        help="Forwarded to package.py for environments without live connector access",
+    )
+
+    status_parser = subparsers.add_parser("status", help="Read backend status for a Pack Test run")
+    status_parser.add_argument("--test-run-id", help="Defaults to data/.pack-publish-state.json")
+
+    submit_parser = subparsers.add_parser("submit", help="Promote accepted test asset and submit to Pack Store")
+    submit_parser.add_argument("--test-run-id", help="Defaults to data/.pack-publish-state.json")
+    submit_parser.add_argument("--pack-id", help="Use an existing Pack Store pack id")
+
+    return parser
+
+
+class BackendClient:
+    def __init__(self, base_url: str, token: str):
+        self.base_url = base_url.rstrip("/")
+        self.token = token
+
+    @classmethod
+    def from_env(cls) -> "BackendClient":
+        base_url = (
+            os.environ.get("CLAW_INTERFACE_URL")
+            or os.environ.get("ECAP_CLAW_INTERFACE_URL")
+            or ""
+        ).strip()
+        if not base_url:
+            raise PublishError("CLAW_INTERFACE_URL is required")
+        token = os.environ.get("USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN", "").strip()
+        if not token:
+            raise PublishError("USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN is required")
+        return cls(base_url, token)
+
+    def json(self, method: str, path: str, body: dict | None = None, timeout: float = 60.0) -> dict:
+        data = None
+        headers = {
+            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.token}",
+            "Accept": "application/json",
+            "User-Agent": "agent-studio-publish/1.0",
+        }
+        if body is not None:
+            data = json.dumps(body, ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")
+            headers["Content-Type"] = "application/json"
+        req = request.Request(self.base_url + path, data=data, headers=headers, method=method)
+        return self._send(req, timeout=timeout)
+
+    def _send(self, req: request.Request, timeout: float) -> dict:
+        try:
+            with request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:
+                raw = resp.read().decode("utf-8")
+        except error.HTTPError as exc:
+            raw = exc.read().decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
+            message = extract_error_message(raw) or exc.reason
+            raise PublishError(f"Backend returned HTTP {exc.code}: {message}") from exc
+        except error.URLError as exc:
+            raise PublishError(f"Backend request failed: {exc.reason}") from exc
+
+        if not raw.strip():
+            return {}
+        try:
+            return json.loads(raw)
+        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
+            raise PublishError(f"Backend returned non-JSON response: {raw[:200]}") from exc
+
+
+class R2UploadClient:
+    def __init__(self, upload_url: str, token: str):
+        self.upload_url = normalize_upload_url(upload_url)
+        self.token = token
+
+    @classmethod
+    def from_env(cls, token: str) -> "R2UploadClient":
+        upload_url = os.environ.get("R2_AGENT_PACKS_UPLOAD_URL", "").strip()
+        if not upload_url:
+            raise PublishError("R2_AGENT_PACKS_UPLOAD_URL is required")
+        return cls(upload_url, token)
+
+    def upload_archive(
+        self,
+        *,
+        archive_path: Path,
+        pack_id: str,
+        content_type: str,
+        timeout: float = 120.0,
+    ) -> dict:
+        body, boundary = build_multipart_body(
+            {"pack_id": pack_id, "filename": archive_path.name},
+            file_field="file",
+            file_path=archive_path,
+            content_type=content_type,
+        )
+        req = request.Request(
+            self.upload_url,
+            data=body,
+            headers={
+                "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.token}",
+                "Accept": "application/json",
+                "Content-Type": f"multipart/form-data; boundary={boundary}",
+                "User-Agent": "agent-studio-publish/1.0",
+            },
+            method="POST",
+        )
+        try:
+            with request.urlopen(req, timeout=timeout) as resp:
+                raw = resp.read().decode("utf-8")
+        except error.HTTPError as exc:
+            raw = exc.read().decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
+            message = extract_error_message(raw) or exc.reason
+            raise PublishError(f"R2 upload worker returned HTTP {exc.code}: {message}") from exc
+        except error.URLError as exc:
+            raise PublishError(f"R2 upload worker request failed: {exc.reason}") from exc
+
+        if not raw.strip():
+            return {}
+        try:
+            return json.loads(raw)
+        except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
+            raise PublishError(f"R2 upload worker returned non-JSON response: {raw[:200]}") from exc
+
+
+def normalize_upload_url(value: str) -> str:
+    url = value.strip().rstrip("/")
+    if not url:
+        raise PublishError("R2_AGENT_PACKS_UPLOAD_URL is required")
+    if not url.startswith(("http://", "https://")):
+        raise PublishError("R2_AGENT_PACKS_UPLOAD_URL must be an absolute URL")
+    return url if url.endswith("/upload") else f"{url}/upload"
+
+
+def build_multipart_body(
+    fields: dict[str, str],
+    *,
+    file_field: str,
+    file_path: Path,
+    content_type: str,
+) -> tuple[bytes, str]:
+    boundary = f"----agent-studio-{uuid.uuid4().hex}"
+    body = bytearray()
+    for name, value in fields.items():
+        body.extend(f"--{boundary}\r\n".encode("utf-8"))
+        body.extend(f'Content-Disposition: form-data; name="{name}"\r\n\r\n'.encode("utf-8"))
+        body.extend(value.encode("utf-8"))
+        body.extend(b"\r\n")
+    body.extend(f"--{boundary}\r\n".encode("utf-8"))
+    body.extend(
+        (
+            f'Content-Disposition: form-data; name="{file_field}"; '
+            f'filename="{file_path.name}"\r\n'
+            f"Content-Type: {content_type}\r\n\r\n"
+        ).encode("utf-8")
+    )
+    body.extend(file_path.read_bytes())
+    body.extend(b"\r\n")
+    body.extend(f"--{boundary}--\r\n".encode("utf-8"))
+    return bytes(body), boundary
+
+
+def extract_error_message(raw: str) -> str:
+    try:
+        data = json.loads(raw)
+    except json.JSONDecodeError:
+        return raw[:500]
+    if isinstance(data, dict):
+        detail = data.get("detail") or data.get("error") or data.get("message")
+        if isinstance(detail, str):
+            return detail
+        if isinstance(detail, dict):
+            return json.dumps(detail, ensure_ascii=False)
+    return raw[:500]
+
+
+def unwrap(data: dict) -> dict:
+    if data.get("success") is True and isinstance(data.get("data"), dict):
+        return data["data"]
+    return data
+
+
+def start_publish(
+    pack_dir: Path,
+    client: BackendClient,
+    upload_client: R2UploadClient,
+    args: argparse.Namespace,
+) -> dict:
+    metadata = read_pack_metadata(pack_dir)
+    archive_info = run_package(pack_dir, Path(args.output), args.skip_connector_validation)
+    if not args.skip_snapshot:
+        run_snapshot(pack_dir, metadata["version"])
+
+    org_id = get_org_id(client)
+    archive_path = Path(archive_info["archive"])
+    archive_sha256 = sha256_file(archive_path)
+    content_type = archive_content_type(archive_path)
+    uploaded_archive = upload_client.upload_archive(
+        archive_path=archive_path,
+        pack_id=metadata["display_id"],
+        content_type=content_type,
+    )
+    test_run = create_test_run(
+        client=client,
+        org_id=org_id,
+        metadata=metadata,
+        archive_path=archive_path,
+        archive_sha256=archive_sha256,
+        content_type=content_type,
+        uploaded_archive=uploaded_archive,
+    )
+    final_run = wait_for_preview(
+        client=client,
+        org_id=org_id,
+        test_run_id=test_run["test_run_id"],
+        timeout=args.poll_timeout,
+        interval=args.poll_interval,
+    )
+
+    state = {
+        "org_id": org_id,
+        "pack_name": metadata["pack_name"],
+        "display_id": metadata["display_id"],
+        "version": metadata["version"],
+        "archive": archive_info["archive"],
+        "archive_sha256": archive_sha256,
+        "archive_key": uploaded_archive.get("key"),
+        "test_run_id": test_run["test_run_id"],
+        "preview_url": final_run.get("preview_url"),
+        "status": final_run.get("status"),
+    }
+    write_state(pack_dir, state)
+
+    return {
+        "status": "ok" if final_run.get("status") in {"ready_for_preview", "previewing", "accepted"} else "pending",
+        "pack_name": metadata["pack_name"],
+        "version": metadata["version"],
+        "org_id": org_id,
+        "test_run_id": test_run["test_run_id"],
+        "run_status": final_run.get("status"),
+        "archive": archive_info["archive"],
+        "archive_sha256": state["archive_sha256"],
+        "archive_key": state["archive_key"],
+        "preview_url": final_run.get("preview_url"),
+        "reused_test_run": False,
+        "state_file": str(pack_dir / STATE_REL),
+        "next": "Open preview_url, accept the run, then run publish.py submit.",
+    }
+
+
+def status_publish(pack_dir: Path, client: BackendClient, args: argparse.Namespace) -> dict:
+    state = read_state(pack_dir)
+    org_id = state.get("org_id") or get_org_id(client)
+    test_run_id = args.test_run_id or state.get("test_run_id")
+    if not test_run_id:
+        raise PublishError("No test_run_id provided and no saved publish state exists")
+    run = get_test_run(client, org_id, test_run_id)
+    state.update({"org_id": org_id, "test_run_id": test_run_id, "status": run.get("status"), "preview_url": run.get("preview_url")})
+    write_state(pack_dir, state)
+    return {
+        "status": "ok",
+        "org_id": org_id,
+        "test_run_id": test_run_id,
+        "run_status": run.get("status"),
+        "preview_url": run.get("preview_url"),
+        "failure_code": run.get("failure_code"),
+        "failure_summary": run.get("failure_summary"),
+    }
+
+
+def submit_publish(pack_dir: Path, client: BackendClient, args: argparse.Namespace) -> dict:
+    state = read_state(pack_dir)
+    metadata = read_pack_metadata(pack_dir)
+    org_id = state.get("org_id") or get_org_id(client)
+    test_run_id = args.test_run_id or state.get("test_run_id")
+    if not test_run_id:
+        raise PublishError("No test_run_id provided and no saved publish state exists")
+
+    run = get_test_run(client, org_id, test_run_id)
+    if run.get("status") not in SUBMIT_READY_STATUSES:
+        raise PublishError(
+            f"Pack Test run is {run.get('status')!r}; accept it in the preview before submitting."
+        )
+
+    pack = get_or_create_pack(client, org_id, metadata, args.pack_id)
+    promoted = client.json(
+        "POST",
+        f"/orgs/{org_id}/packs/{pack['pack_id']}/assets/promote",
+        {"test_run_id": test_run_id},
+    )
+    submission = client.json(
+        "POST",
+        f"/orgs/{org_id}/packs/{pack['pack_id']}/submissions",
+        {
+            "pack_version": metadata["version"],
+            "parent_version_id": pack.get("latest_submission_id") or None,
+            "asset_id": promoted["asset_id"],
+            "test_run_id": test_run_id,
+            "category": metadata["category"],
+            "short_bio": metadata["short_bio"],
+            "bio": metadata["bio"],
+            "skills": metadata["skills"],
+            "integrations": metadata["integrations"],
+            "automations": metadata["automations"],
+            "schema_validated": True,
+        },
+    )
+
+    state.update(
+        {
+            "org_id": org_id,
+            "test_run_id": test_run_id,
+            "status": "submitted",
+            "pack_id": pack["pack_id"],
+            "asset_id": promoted["asset_id"],
+            "submission_id": submission["submission_id"],
+        }
+    )
+    write_state(pack_dir, state)
+    write_delivery_state(
+        pack_dir=pack_dir,
+        method=DELIVERY_METHOD_PACK_STORE,
+        pack_name=metadata["pack_name"],
+        version=metadata["version"],
+        test_run_id=test_run_id,
+        pack_id=pack["pack_id"],
+        asset_id=promoted["asset_id"],
+        submission_id=submission["submission_id"],
+    )
+
+    return {
+        "status": "ok",
+        "org_id": org_id,
+        "pack_id": pack["pack_id"],
+        "test_run_id": test_run_id,
+        "asset_id": promoted["asset_id"],
+        "archive_sha256": promoted["archive_sha256"],
+        "submission_id": submission["submission_id"],
+        "submission_status": submission["status"],
+        "state_file": str(pack_dir / STATE_REL),
+    }
+
+
+def read_pack_metadata(pack_dir: Path) -> dict:
+    manifest_path = find_pack_manifest(pack_dir)
+    if manifest_path is None:
+        raise FileNotFoundError("agent/agent-pack.yaml not found")
+    manifest = manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+    pack_name = extract_field(manifest, "name")
+    version = extract_field(manifest, "version")
+    if not pack_name or not version:
+        raise PublishError("agent/agent-pack.yaml must include name and version")
+
+    description = read_description_json(pack_dir) or {}
+    display_id = str(description.get("agentPack_id") or pack_name)
+    return {
+        "pack_name": pack_name,
+        "display_id": display_id,
+        "version": version,
+        "listing_metadata": description,
+        "quick_commands": parse_quick_commands(manifest),
+        "smoke_scenarios": [],
+        "avatar_url": public_url_or_none(description.get("avatar_url")),
+        "category": optional_string(description.get("category")),
+        "short_bio": optional_string(description.get("short_bio")),
+        "bio": optional_string(description.get("bio")),
+        "skills": optional_string_list(description.get("skills")),
+        "integrations": optional_string_list(description.get("integrations")),
+        "automations": optional_string_list(description.get("automations")),
+    }
+
+
+def run_package(pack_dir: Path, output_dir: Path, skip_connector_validation: bool) -> dict:
+    if not output_dir.is_absolute():
+        output_dir = pack_dir.resolve() / output_dir
+    cmd = [sys.executable, str(PACKAGE_SCRIPT), "--output", str(output_dir)]
+    if skip_connector_validation:
+        cmd.append("--skip-connector-validation")
+    proc = subprocess.run(cmd, cwd=str(pack_dir), capture_output=True, text=True, check=False)
+    if proc.returncode != 0:
+        message = proc.stderr.strip() or proc.stdout.strip() or "package.py failed"
+        raise PublishError(message)
+    try:
+        payload = json.loads(proc.stdout)
+    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
+        raise PublishError(f"package.py returned non-JSON output: {proc.stdout[:500]}") from exc
+    if payload.get("status") != "ok" or not payload.get("archive"):
+        raise PublishError(payload.get("message") or "package.py did not produce an archive")
+    return payload
+
+
+def run_snapshot(pack_dir: Path, version: str) -> None:
+    proc = subprocess.run(
+        [sys.executable, str(SNAPSHOT_SCRIPT), "capture", "--version", version],
+        cwd=str(pack_dir),
+        capture_output=True,
+        text=True,
+        check=False,
+    )
+    if proc.returncode != 0:
+        raise PublishError(proc.stderr.strip() or proc.stdout.strip() or "snapshot.py capture failed")
+
+
+def archive_content_type(archive_path: Path) -> str:
+    name = archive_path.name.lower()
+    if name.endswith(".tar.gz"):
+        return "application/gzip"
+    if name.endswith(".zip"):
+        return "application/zip"
+    raise PublishError("Pack Test archive must be .tar.gz or .zip")
+
+
+def create_test_run(
+    client: BackendClient,
+    org_id: str,
+    metadata: dict,
+    archive_path: Path,
+    archive_sha256: str,
+    content_type: str,
+    uploaded_archive: dict,
+) -> dict:
+    archive_key = uploaded_archive.get("key")
+    if not isinstance(archive_key, str) or not archive_key.strip():
+        raise PublishError("R2 upload worker response missing key")
+    archive_size = uploaded_archive.get("size")
+    if not isinstance(archive_size, int):
+        archive_size = archive_path.stat().st_size
+    body = {
+        "pack_name": metadata["pack_name"],
+        "display_id": metadata["display_id"],
+        "pack_version": metadata["version"],
+        "archive": {
+            "key": archive_key,
+            "name": archive_path.name,
+            "size": archive_size,
+            "sha256": archive_sha256,
+            "content_type": content_type,
+            "manifest_name": metadata["pack_name"],
+            "manifest_version": metadata["version"],
+        },
+        "listing_metadata": metadata["listing_metadata"],
+        "quick_commands": metadata["quick_commands"],
+        "smoke_scenarios": metadata["smoke_scenarios"],
+    }
+    return client.json("POST", f"/orgs/{org_id}/pack-test-runs", body)
+
+
+def wait_for_preview(client: BackendClient, org_id: str, test_run_id: str, timeout: float, interval: float) -> dict:
+    deadline = time.time() + max(timeout, 0)
+    latest = get_test_run(client, org_id, test_run_id)
+    while latest.get("status") not in TERMINAL_PREVIEW_STATUSES and time.time() < deadline:
+        print(f"pack test status: {latest.get('status')}", file=sys.stderr)
+        time.sleep(max(interval, 1.0))
+        latest = get_test_run(client, org_id, test_run_id)
+    return latest
+
+
+def get_test_run(client: BackendClient, org_id: str, test_run_id: str) -> dict:
+    return client.json("GET", f"/orgs/{org_id}/pack-test-runs/{test_run_id}")
+
+
+def get_org_id(client: BackendClient) -> str:
+    account = unwrap(client.json("GET", "/account/me"))
+    org = account.get("org") if isinstance(account.get("org"), dict) else {}
+    org_id = org.get("org_id") or account.get("org_id")
+    if not org_id:
+        raise PublishError("/account/me did not return org.org_id")
+    return str(org_id)
+
+
+def get_or_create_pack(client: BackendClient, org_id: str, metadata: dict, pack_id: str | None) -> dict:
+    if pack_id:
+        return client.json("GET", f"/orgs/{org_id}/packs/{pack_id}")
+
+    listed = client.json("GET", f"/orgs/{org_id}/packs")
+    packs = listed.get("packs") if isinstance(listed.get("packs"), list) else []
+    for pack in packs:
+        if pack.get("display_id") == metadata["display_id"] or pack.get("pack_name") == metadata["pack_name"]:
+            if pack.get("status") == "deprecated":
+                raise PublishError(f"Pack {pack['pack_id']} is deprecated")
+            return pack
+
+    return client.json(
+        "POST",
+        f"/orgs/{org_id}/packs",
+        {
+            "pack_name": metadata["pack_name"],
+            "display_id": metadata["display_id"],
+            "avatar_url": metadata["avatar_url"],
+            "sort_order": None,
+            "category": metadata["category"],
+            "short_bio": metadata["short_bio"],
+            "bio": metadata["bio"],
+            "skills": metadata["skills"],
+            "integrations": metadata["integrations"],
+            "automations": metadata["automations"],
+        },
+    )
+
+
+def parse_quick_commands(manifest: str) -> list[dict]:
+    header_match = re.search(r"^quick_commands:(.*)$", manifest, re.MULTILINE)
+    if not header_match:
+        return []
+    inline = header_match.group(1).strip()
+    if re.fullmatch(r"\[\]\s*(?:#.*)?", inline):
+        return []
+    if inline and not inline.startswith("#"):
+        return []
+
+    section_lines = manifest[header_match.end():].split("\n")[1:]
+    commands = []
+    current = None
+    for line in section_lines:
+        if line and not line[0].isspace() and not line.startswith("#"):
+            break
+        stripped = line.strip()
+        if not stripped or stripped.startswith("#"):
+            continue
+        entry_match = re.fullmatch(r"  - id:\s+(.*)", line)
+        if entry_match:
+            current = {"id": strip_yaml_scalar(entry_match.group(1))}
+            commands.append(current)
+            continue
+        field_match = re.fullmatch(r"    (label|label_cn|prompt):\s+(.*)", line)
+        if field_match and current is not None:
+            field, raw = field_match.groups()
+            current[field] = strip_yaml_scalar(raw)
+    return commands
+
+
+def strip_yaml_scalar(raw: str) -> str:
+    raw = re.split(r"\s+#", raw.strip(), maxsplit=1)[0].strip()
+    if raw.startswith('"'):
+        try:
+            value, _end = json.JSONDecoder().raw_decode(raw)
+            return value if isinstance(value, str) else raw
+        except json.JSONDecodeError:
+            return raw.strip('"')
+    for quote in ("'", '"'):
+        if len(raw) >= 2 and raw.startswith(quote) and raw.endswith(quote):
+            return raw[1:-1]
+    return raw
+
+
+def optional_string(value) -> str | None:
+    return value if isinstance(value, str) and value.strip() else None
+
+
+def optional_string_list(value) -> list[str] | None:
+    if not isinstance(value, list):
+        return None
+    items = [item.strip() for item in value if isinstance(item, str) and item.strip()]
+    return items or None
+
+
+def public_url_or_none(value) -> str | None:
+    if not isinstance(value, str):
+        return None
+    return value if value.startswith("https://") or value.startswith("http://") else None
+
+
+def sha256_file(path: Path) -> str:
+    digest = hashlib.sha256()
+    with path.open("rb") as fh:
+        for chunk in iter(lambda: fh.read(1024 * 1024), b""):
+            digest.update(chunk)
+    return digest.hexdigest()
+
+
+def read_state(pack_dir: Path) -> dict:
+    path = pack_dir / STATE_REL
+    if not path.exists():
+        return {}
+    data = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+    return data if isinstance(data, dict) else {}
+
+
+def write_state(pack_dir: Path, state: dict) -> None:
+    path = pack_dir / STATE_REL
+    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    path.write_text(json.dumps(state, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

+"""Tests for publish.py Pack Test and Pack Store workflow."""
+
+import argparse
+import hashlib
+import json
+import shutil
+import sys
+import tempfile
+import unittest
+from pathlib import Path
+from unittest import mock
+
+SCRIPTS_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
+sys.path.insert(0, str(SCRIPTS_DIR))
+
+import publish  # noqa: E402
+
+
+def _args(**overrides):
+    data = {
+        "output": "zip/",
+        "skip_connector_validation": False,
+        "skip_snapshot": False,
+        "poll_timeout": 0,
+        "poll_interval": 1,
+        "test_run_id": None,
+        "pack_id": None,
+    }
+    data.update(overrides)
+    return argparse.Namespace(**data)
+
+
+def _write_pack(pack_dir: Path) -> None:
+    (pack_dir / "agent").mkdir(parents=True)
+    (pack_dir / "agent" / "agent-pack.yaml").write_text(
+        "\n".join(
+            [
+                "name: worldcup-scout",
+                "version: 0.1.0",
+                "quick_commands:",
+                "  - id: all_features",
+                "    label: All features",
+                "    label_cn: All features",
+                "    prompt: Show all features",
+                "",
+            ]
+        ),
+        encoding="utf-8",
+    )
+    (pack_dir / "agent" / "description.json").write_text(
+        json.dumps(
+            {
+                "agentPack_id": "worldcup-scout",
+                "category": "lifestyle",
+                "short_bio": "World Cup scouting helper.",
+                "bio": "Tracks World Cup context and scouting workflows.",
+                "skills": ["Scouting"],
+                "integrations": ["Web"],
+                "automations": ["Daily digest"],
+            }
+        ),
+        encoding="utf-8",
+    )
+
+
+class FakeUploadClient:
+    def __init__(self, events: list[tuple], key: str = "org_a/worldcup-scout/upload.tar.gz"):
+        self.events = events
+        self.key = key
+
+    def upload_archive(self, *, archive_path: Path, pack_id: str, content_type: str, timeout: float = 120.0) -> dict:
+        self.events.append(("upload", pack_id, archive_path.name, content_type))
+        return {"key": self.key, "size": archive_path.stat().st_size, "url": f"https://r2.test/{self.key}"}
+
+
+class FakeBackendClient:
+    token = "creator-token"
+
+    def __init__(self, events: list[tuple]):
+        self.events = events
+
+    def json(self, method: str, path: str, body: dict | None = None, timeout: float = 60.0) -> dict:
+        self.events.append((method, path, body))
+        if method == "GET" and path == "/account/me":
+            return {"org": {"org_id": "org_a", "status": "active"}}
+        if method == "POST" and path == "/orgs/org_a/pack-test-runs":
+            return {
+                "test_run_id": "ptr_new",
+                "org_id": "org_a",
+                "status": "asset_received",
+                "archive_sha256": body["archive"]["sha256"],
+                "created_at": "2026-06-12T09:00:00+00:00",
+            }
+        if method == "GET" and path == "/orgs/org_a/pack-test-runs/ptr_new":
+            return {
+                "test_run_id": "ptr_new",
+                "status": "ready_for_preview",
+                "preview_url": "https://app.test/chat?computer_id=bot_1&agent_id=main",
+            }
+        raise AssertionError(f"unexpected backend call: {method} {path}")
+
+
+class SubmitBackendClient:
+    token = "creator-token"
+
+    def __init__(self, run_status: str = "accepted"):
+        self.run_status = run_status
+        self.calls: list[tuple] = []
+
+    def json(self, method: str, path: str, body: dict | None = None, timeout: float = 60.0) -> dict:
+        self.calls.append((method, path, body))
+        if method == "GET" and path == "/orgs/org_a/pack-test-runs/ptr_1":
+            return {"test_run_id": "ptr_1", "status": self.run_status}
+        if method == "GET" and path == "/orgs/org_a/packs":
+            return {
+                "packs": [
+                    {
+                        "pack_id": "pack_existing",
+                        "pack_name": "worldcup-scout",
+                        "display_id": "worldcup-scout",
+                        "status": "active",
+                        "latest_submission_id": "sub_old",
+                    }
+                ]
+            }
+        if method == "POST" and path == "/orgs/org_a/packs/pack_existing/assets/promote":
+            return {
+                "asset_id": "org_a/pack_existing/archive.tar.gz",
+                "archive_sha256": "a" * 64,
+                "source_test_run_id": "ptr_1",
+            }
+        if method == "POST" and path == "/orgs/org_a/packs/pack_existing/submissions":
+            return {
+                "submission_id": "sub_new",
+                "pack_id": "pack_existing",
+                "pack_version": body["pack_version"],
+                "status": "submitted",
+                "submitted_at": "2026-06-12T09:30:00+00:00",
+            }
+        raise AssertionError(f"unexpected backend call: {method} {path}")
+
+
+class TestPublishStart(unittest.TestCase):
+    def setUp(self):
+        self.tmp = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="agent-studio-publish-"))
+        _write_pack(self.tmp)
+        self.archive = self.tmp / "zip" / "worldcup-scout-0.1.0.tar.gz"
+        self.archive.parent.mkdir()
+        self.archive.write_bytes(b"archive-bytes")
+
+    def tearDown(self):
+        shutil.rmtree(self.tmp)
+
+    def test_start_uploads_to_r2_worker_then_creates_json_test_run(self):
+        archive_sha256 = hashlib.sha256(self.archive.read_bytes()).hexdigest()
+        (self.tmp / "data").mkdir()
+        (self.tmp / "data" / ".pack-publish-state.json").write_text(
+            json.dumps(
+                {
+                    "org_id": "org_a",
+                    "pack_name": "worldcup-scout",
+                    "display_id": "worldcup-scout",
+                    "version": "0.1.0",
+                    "archive_sha256": archive_sha256,
+                    "test_run_id": "ptr_old",
+                }
+            ),
+            encoding="utf-8",
+        )
+        events: list[tuple] = []
+
+        with (
+            mock.patch("publish.run_package", return_value={"status": "ok", "archive": str(self.archive)}),
+            mock.patch("publish.run_snapshot") as run_snapshot,
+        ):
+            result = publish.start_publish(self.tmp, FakeBackendClient(events), FakeUploadClient(events), _args())
+
+        self.assertEqual(result["test_run_id"], "ptr_new")
+        self.assertFalse(result["reused_test_run"])
+        run_snapshot.assert_called_once_with(self.tmp, "0.1.0")
+        self.assertEqual(events[0], ("GET", "/account/me", None))
+        self.assertEqual(events[1], ("upload", "worldcup-scout", self.archive.name, "application/gzip"))
+        self.assertEqual(events[2][0:2], ("POST", "/orgs/org_a/pack-test-runs"))
+        self.assertNotIn("ptr_old", json.dumps(events), "start must not reuse a saved Pack Test run")
+
+        create_body = events[2][2]
+        self.assertEqual(create_body["pack_name"], "worldcup-scout")
+        self.assertEqual(create_body["display_id"], "worldcup-scout")
+        self.assertEqual(create_body["pack_version"], "0.1.0")
+        self.assertEqual(create_body["archive"]["key"], "org_a/worldcup-scout/upload.tar.gz")
+        self.assertEqual(create_body["archive"]["name"], self.archive.name)
+        self.assertEqual(create_body["archive"]["size"], self.archive.stat().st_size)
+        self.assertEqual(create_body["archive"]["sha256"], archive_sha256)
+        self.assertEqual(create_body["archive"]["content_type"], "application/gzip")
+        self.assertEqual(create_body["archive"]["manifest_name"], "worldcup-scout")
+        self.assertEqual(create_body["archive"]["manifest_version"], "0.1.0")
+        self.assertIsInstance(create_body["listing_metadata"], dict)
+        self.assertEqual(create_body["quick_commands"][0]["id"], "all_features")
+
+        saved_state = json.loads((self.tmp / "data" / ".pack-publish-state.json").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+        self.assertEqual(saved_state["test_run_id"], "ptr_new")
+        self.assertEqual(saved_state["archive_key"], "org_a/worldcup-scout/upload.tar.gz")
+
+    def test_create_test_run_requires_upload_key(self):
+        with self.assertRaisesRegex(publish.PublishError, "missing key"):
+            publish.create_test_run(
+                client=FakeBackendClient([]),
+                org_id="org_a",
+                metadata=publish.read_pack_metadata(self.tmp),
+                archive_path=self.archive,
+                archive_sha256="a" * 64,
+                content_type="application/gzip",
+                uploaded_archive={},
+            )
+
+
+class TestPublishSubmit(unittest.TestCase):
+    def setUp(self):
+        self.tmp = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="agent-studio-submit-"))
+        _write_pack(self.tmp)
+        (self.tmp / "data").mkdir()
+        (self.tmp / "data" / ".pack-publish-state.json").write_text(
+            json.dumps({"org_id": "org_a", "test_run_id": "ptr_1"}),
+            encoding="utf-8",
+        )
+
+    def tearDown(self):
+        shutil.rmtree(self.tmp)
+
+    def test_submit_promotes_accepted_run_and_creates_submission(self):
+        backend = SubmitBackendClient(run_status="accepted")
+
+        result = publish.submit_publish(self.tmp, backend, _args())
+
+        self.assertEqual(result["submission_id"], "sub_new")
+        self.assertEqual(
+            [call[0:2] for call in backend.calls],
+            [
+                ("GET", "/orgs/org_a/pack-test-runs/ptr_1"),
+                ("GET", "/orgs/org_a/packs"),
+                ("POST", "/orgs/org_a/packs/pack_existing/assets/promote"),
+                ("POST", "/orgs/org_a/packs/pack_existing/submissions"),
+            ],
+        )
+        submission_body = backend.calls[-1][2]
+        self.assertEqual(submission_body["asset_id"], "org_a/pack_existing/archive.tar.gz")
+        self.assertEqual(submission_body["test_run_id"], "ptr_1")
+        self.assertEqual(submission_body["parent_version_id"], "sub_old")
+        self.assertTrue(submission_body["schema_validated"])
+
+    def test_submit_requires_accepted_run(self):
+        backend = SubmitBackendClient(run_status="previewing")
+
+        with self.assertRaisesRegex(publish.PublishError, "accept it in the preview"):
+            publish.submit_publish(self.tmp, backend, _args())
+
+        self.assertEqual([call[0:2] for call in backend.calls], [("GET", "/orgs/org_a/pack-test-runs/ptr_1")])
+
+
+class TestPublishConfig(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_upload_url_accepts_base_or_full_upload_url(self):
+        self.assertEqual(publish.normalize_upload_url("https://worker.test"), "https://worker.test/upload")
+        self.assertEqual(publish.normalize_upload_url("https://worker.test/upload"), "https://worker.test/upload")
+
+    def test_backend_client_does_not_use_frontend_url_as_backend(self):
+        with mock.patch.dict(
+            "os.environ",
+            {"APP_PUBLIC_URL": "https://app.test", "USER_INTERNAL_TOKEN": "tok"},
+            clear=True,
+        ):
+            with self.assertRaisesRegex(publish.PublishError, "CLAW_INTERFACE_URL"):
+                publish.BackendClient.from_env()
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()

@@ -40,4 +40,4 @@ Persisted in `.mode`. Default **DEV**. Creator can switch via `/dev` or `/test`.
 
 ## Commands
 
-`/dev` / `/test` switch mode (above). All `/studio *` commands are defined in `.agents/skills/agent-studio/SKILL.md` "Studio commands".
+`/dev` / `/test` switch mode (above). `/test` is only the local in-workspace simulation mode for the pack under `agent/`; it does not upload an archive, create a Pack Test run, install a temporary bot, or submit anything. Full Pack Test starts with `/studio publish`. All `/studio *` commands are defined in `.agents/skills/agent-studio/SKILL.md` "Studio commands".
